title: erigeron × favratii
intro: Erigeron × favratii, also known as the Alpine Fleabane or Alpine Daisy, is a hybrid plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Erigeron × favratii include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine. Erigeron × favratii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used topically to treat wounds, skin irritations, and insect bites.
